report_metadata.cancer_type: Carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma (CEPA) of the parapharyngeal space
molecular_profile: Malignant transformation of long-standing (20-year) pleomorphic adenoma

treatments.name: Wide Surgical Excision + Adjuvant Intensity-Modulated Radiation Therapy (IMRT)

evidence_level.rationale: Standard of care supported by NCCN guidelines for salivary gland malignancies and multiple large retrospective series. National Cancer Database analysis (n>7000) confirms adjuvant RT survival benefit. Not Phase 3 RCT due to rarity, but the highest level of evidence available for this tumor type

survival_benefit.rationale: Surgery plus adjuvant RT achieves 5-year local control of 75% vs 49% with surgery alone. 5-year OS for stage III CEPA approximately 50-60% with combined modality treatment. Multi-institutional CEPA cohort shows 5-year OS of 61.7% overall; adjuvant RT significantly improves locoregional control

accessibility.rationale: Standard of care available at all head and neck cancer centers worldwide. IMRT technology widely available. Parapharyngeal space resection requires experienced skull base surgeon but is performed at tertiary centers

safety_profile.rationale: Parapharyngeal space surgery carries risk of cranial nerve VII (facial nerve) and X (vagus) injury — the most common surgical complications. Possible first-bite syndrome, Frey syndrome. RT causes xerostomia, mucositis, dysphagia, osteoradionecrosis risk. Dose 60-66 Gy over 6-7 weeks

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability — surgery plus RT is indicated for all CEPA regardless of molecular profile. PLAG1 rearrangement confirms origin from pleomorphic adenoma, supporting diagnosis. Ki-67 30% indicates aggressive biology requiring adjuvant treatment

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical resection removes the primary tumor and involved lymph nodes (neck dissection for N1 disease). Adjuvant IMRT delivers 60-66 Gy to the tumor bed and regional lymphatics to eradicate microscopic residual disease. IMRT allows conformal dose delivery while sparing adjacent critical structures including the contralateral parotid, brainstem, and spinal cord.

key_evidence.study_name: National Cancer Database Analysis — Adjuvant RT for Salivary Gland Malignancies

biomarker_requirements: None — universally applicable
notable_side_effects: Cranial nerve VII injury / facial paralysis (most common PPS surgical complication)
treatments.availability: Standard of Care — available at all major head and neck cancer centers

treatments.name: Larotrectinib (TRK Inhibitor — for NTRK Fusion-Positive Cases)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 trials with 92% ORR in TRK fusion-positive salivary gland cancers. FDA approved tissue-agnostically for NTRK fusion-positive tumors. Median duration of response 35.2 months. Strong evidence in biomarker-selected population

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 92% with median DOR 35.2 months in TRK fusion-positive SGC — dramatically exceeds any chemotherapy. Deep and durable responses. However, benefit depends entirely on presence of NTRK fusion which is not confirmed in this patient

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved since 2018 for NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors. Oral administration. Available commercially but requires molecular confirmation of NTRK fusion. Companion diagnostic testing via NGS or FISH needed

safety_profile.rationale: Well tolerated with low rate of serious adverse events. Most common AEs: fatigue, dizziness, nausea, constipation, diarrhea — predominantly grade 1-2. Rare neurotoxicity. Favorable safety profile compared to chemotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires NTRK gene fusion which is NOT confirmed in this patient. PLAG1 rearrangement is distinct from NTRK fusions. NTRK fusions occur in approximately 2-4% of salivary gland cancers. NGS testing recommended to rule in/out NTRK fusion as secondary event in CEPA

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Larotrectinib is a highly selective oral inhibitor of tropomyosin receptor kinases (TRK) A, B, and C, encoded by NTRK1, NTRK2, and NTRK3 genes. In tumors harboring NTRK gene fusions, constitutively active TRK fusion proteins drive oncogenic signaling through RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT pathways. Larotrectinib blocks this aberrant signaling.

key_evidence.study_name: Larotrectinib in TRK Fusion-Positive Salivary Gland Cancers

biomarker_requirements: NTRK1, NTRK2, or NTRK3 gene fusion (confirmed by NGS or FISH)
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (tissue-agnostic for NTRK fusion-positive tumors since 2018)

treatments.name: Comprehensive Molecular Profiling (NGS) + Matched Targeted Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN and ESMO guidelines recommend NGS for advanced salivary gland carcinomas. Actionable targets found in 20-40% of cases. ASCO/ESMO recommend NTRK testing for all solid tumors. Growing evidence base for matched therapy in rare tumors

survival_benefit.rationale: Identifying actionable targets (NTRK fusions, HER2 amplification, BRAF mutations, AR expression, PIK3CA mutations) enables matched therapies with significantly higher response rates. Up to 92% ORR if NTRK fusion found. Value is in finding the right therapy rather than direct survival benefit

accessibility.rationale: NGS panels (FoundationOne, Tempus, Caris) widely available at academic and community oncology centers. Turnaround time 2-3 weeks. Cost $3000-5000 but frequently covered by insurance for advanced cancers. PLAG1 already confirmed — additional genomic profiling may reveal secondary hits

safety_profile.rationale: Diagnostic procedure with no direct safety risk. Requires tumor tissue (biopsy or surgical specimen) or liquid biopsy (ctDNA). No adverse effects from testing itself

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly addresses biomarker identification. CEPA can harbor secondary molecular events beyond PLAG1: TP53 mutations, ERBB2 amplification, PIK3CA mutations, CDKN2A loss, NTRK fusions. Comprehensive profiling maximizes chance of finding actionable target

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Next-generation sequencing panels interrogate hundreds of cancer-relevant genes simultaneously, identifying point mutations, insertions/deletions, copy number alterations, and gene fusions. For CEPA specifically, this can detect secondary oncogenic events that drove malignant transformation beyond the founding PLAG1 rearrangement, such as TP53 mutations, ERBB2 (HER2) amplification, PIK3CA mutations, CDKN2A deletions, or NTRK fusions — each of which has a matched FDA-approved or investigational targeted therapy.

key_evidence.study_name: Targeted Therapy in SGC — Prevalence of Actionable Molecular Alterations

biomarker_requirements: Sufficient tumor tissue or circulating tumor DNA for NGS analysis
treatments.availability: Widely available through commercial NGS platforms (FoundationOne, Tempus, Caris, Guardant360)

treatments.name: Transoral Robotic Surgery (TORS) for Parapharyngeal Space Tumors

evidence_level.rationale: Case reports and small case series only. First reported TORS for parapharyngeal CEPA published 2022. Growing institutional experience but no comparative trials. Evidence limited by extreme rarity of this presentation

survival_benefit.rationale: Achieves complete resection with negative margins comparable to open approaches. Published case showed disease-free status at 7-month follow-up after TORS plus adjuvant IMRT (54-66 Gy). Reduced morbidity may allow faster recovery and earlier adjuvant therapy

accessibility.rationale: Requires da Vinci robotic system, intraoperative neuro-navigation, and experienced TORS surgeon. Available only at specialized high-volume centers. Not all parapharyngeal tumors are anatomically amenable to TORS

safety_profile.rationale: Minimally invasive with reduced morbidity vs open transcervical or transmandibular approaches. No external incision. Published cases report no swallowing dysfunction or pain. Avoids mandibulotomy and associated complications. Cranial nerve injury risk remains

biomarker_match.rationale: Surgical approach applicable regardless of molecular profile. Suitable for parapharyngeal CEPA arising from pre-existing pleomorphic adenoma with PLAG1 rearrangement

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Transoral robotic surgery uses the da Vinci robotic system to resect parapharyngeal space tumors through the oral cavity, avoiding external incisions. The robotic platform provides 3D magnified visualization, tremor filtration, and articulating instruments that navigate the complex parapharyngeal anatomy. Combined with intraoperative neuro-navigation and ultrasound, critical neurovascular structures (carotid artery, jugular vein, cranial nerves IX-XII) are identified and preserved.

key_evidence.study_name: TORS for Parapharyngeal Carcinoma Ex-Pleomorphic Adenoma: Case Report

biomarker_requirements: None — anatomic suitability required rather than biomarker
notable_side_effects: Potential cranial nerve injury (VII, IX, X, XI, XII)
Tumor spillage risk if capsule violated
treatments.availability: Available at specialized robotic surgery centers with TORS experience

treatments.name: Platinum + Docetaxel Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II level data showing ORR 46% and median OS 28.2 months in salivary gland carcinoma — among the highest reported response rates for any chemotherapy doublet. Small sample sizes

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 46% and median OS 28.2 months represents one of the best chemotherapy outcomes reported in salivary gland cancer. Potentially superior to CAP and carboplatin-paclitaxel. Primarily evaluated in recurrent/metastatic setting

accessibility.rationale: Both agents generic and widely available. Standard oncology drugs administered at any infusion center

safety_profile.rationale: Cisplatin nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, nausea. Docetaxel causes neutropenia, fluid retention, peripheral neuropathy, hypersensitivity. Requires premedication with dexamethasone. Moderate toxicity profile

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-specific. Active across salivary gland subtypes. Ki-67 30% indicates high proliferative index potentially responsive to taxane-platinum therapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cisplatin induces DNA crosslinks that block replication and activate apoptosis. Docetaxel binds beta-tubulin, stabilizing microtubules and preventing depolymerization required for cell division. The combination provides complementary DNA- and microtubule-directed cytotoxicity.
biomarker_requirements: None

treatments.name: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (Leuprorelin + Bicalutamide — Combined Androgen Blockade)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II prospective trial with 36 AR-positive patients showing 42% ORR and 75% clinical benefit rate. Primarily studied in salivary duct carcinoma (94% of enrolled patients). Limited data in CEPA subtype specifically

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 42% with clinical benefit rate 75% and median OS 30.5 months in AR-positive salivary duct carcinoma. Could equal or exceed chemotherapy with less toxicity. However, CEPA is rarely salivary duct carcinoma subtype — AR expression must be confirmed

accessibility.rationale: Both agents generic and inexpensive. Standard prostate cancer drugs with decades of use. Oral bicalutamide plus monthly/quarterly leuprorelin injection. Available at all oncology centers

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well tolerated. Hot flashes, sexual dysfunction, gynecomastia, fatigue, bone density loss. Less toxic than cytotoxic chemotherapy. Long-term cardiovascular and metabolic risks manageable. Good quality of life

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires androgen receptor positivity which is NOT confirmed in this patient. AR expression is common in salivary duct carcinoma (60-90%) but uncommon in most CEPA subtypes. IHC testing for AR expression on tumor tissue is required. PLAG1 rearrangement does not predict AR expression

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combined androgen blockade uses leuprorelin (GnRH agonist) to suppress testicular testosterone production via pituitary desensitization, combined with bicalutamide (anti-androgen) to block residual androgen signaling at the receptor level. In AR-positive salivary gland carcinomas, androgen receptor drives tumor proliferation and survival, making it a therapeutic target analogous to prostate cancer.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Combined Androgen Blockade in AR-Positive SGC

biomarker_requirements: Androgen receptor positive (IHC)
Most commonly applicable to salivary duct carcinoma subtype
treatments.availability: Widely available — generic agents used for prostate cancer

treatments.name: Carboplatin + Paclitaxel Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II data in salivary gland carcinomas. ORR 14-39% reported across studies. Commonly used in clinical practice for recurrent/metastatic salivary gland cancer. No randomized comparison with CAP

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 14-39% with responses generally partial and temporary. Used primarily for palliation in recurrent/metastatic settings. No proven survival benefit as adjuvant therapy beyond surgery plus RT

accessibility.rationale: Both agents generic and widely available. Standard chemotherapy backbone used across oncology. Can be administered at any infusion center

safety_profile.rationale: Better tolerated than CAP — no anthracycline cardiotoxicity concern. Carboplatin causes myelosuppression and nephrotoxicity (less than cisplatin). Paclitaxel causes neuropathy, alopecia, hypersensitivity reactions. Generally manageable toxicity profile

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-specific. Applicable to all salivary gland histologies. Ki-67 30% suggests proliferative tumor potentially responsive to taxane-based therapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Carboplatin forms intrastrand DNA crosslinks disrupting replication. Paclitaxel stabilizes microtubules preventing mitotic spindle disassembly, arresting cells in mitosis. The combination targets DNA integrity and mitotic machinery through complementary mechanisms.
biomarker_requirements: None

treatments.name: Cisplatin + Vinorelbine Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II data showing ORR 31-44% in advanced salivary gland carcinomas. Small sample sizes. One of the more active doublets but no comparative randomized data

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 31-44% with 6-month median PFS. Responses are partial and temporary. Demonstrates activity in salivary gland cancer but limited long-term survival impact in recurrent/metastatic setting

accessibility.rationale: Both agents are generic and widely available. Standard oncology drugs with well-established protocols

safety_profile.rationale: Cisplatin nephro/ototoxicity, vinorelbine myelosuppression and neurotoxicity. Manageable but requires IV administration and monitoring. Better tolerated than CAP overall

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-driven. Applicable to all salivary gland carcinoma subtypes including CEPA. High Ki-67 (30%) may predict chemosensitivity

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cisplatin crosslinks DNA to inhibit replication. Vinorelbine is a vinca alkaloid that binds tubulin, inhibiting microtubule assembly and arresting cells in metaphase. Complementary mechanisms targeting DNA and mitotic apparatus.

key_evidence.study_name: Cisplatin-Vinorelbine in Advanced Salivary Gland Carcinoma

biomarker_requirements: None

treatments.name: Proton Beam Therapy / Carbon Ion Radiotherapy (Particle Beam RT)

evidence_level.rationale: Retrospective series and case reports for salivary gland malignancies. Growing institutional experience particularly in Japan and Germany. Case report of proton beam therapy for myoepithelial CEPA of palate published. No randomized comparisons with IMRT for CEPA

survival_benefit.rationale: Dosimetric advantages allow higher tumor doses (potentially 70-80 GyE) while sparing normal tissues. May improve local control for tumors adjacent to critical structures like brainstem and optic apparatus. Favorable outcomes reported in salivary gland cancer series but no CEPA-specific comparative data

accessibility.rationale: Proton therapy centers limited in number globally (approximately 100 worldwide). Carbon ion facilities extremely rare (fewer than 15 worldwide). Requires referral to specialized center. Significant cost compared to IMRT. May not be covered by all insurers

safety_profile.rationale: Physical advantage of Bragg peak reduces dose to surrounding normal tissues — less xerostomia, less mucositis, less neurotoxicity compared to photon RT. Carbon ions have higher RBE potentially improving tumor control. Reduced late toxicity profile

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-dependent. Applicable to all CEPA regardless of molecular profile. Particularly beneficial for parapharyngeal space tumors adjacent to brainstem, temporal lobe, and cranial nerves where dose sparing is critical

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Proton and carbon ion beams deposit most of their energy at a specific depth (Bragg peak), delivering high doses to the tumor while dramatically reducing exit dose to surrounding tissues. Carbon ions have higher linear energy transfer (LET) and relative biological effectiveness (RBE), producing more DNA double-strand breaks per unit dose. This is particularly advantageous for radioresistant tumors and those near critical structures like the brainstem and cranial nerves in the parapharyngeal space.

key_evidence.study_name: IMPT and Carbon Ion RT in Major Salivary Gland Carcinomas

biomarker_requirements: None — anatomic and dosimetric considerations drive selection
treatments.availability: Limited to specialized proton/carbon ion therapy centers worldwide

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (Anti-PD-1 Immunotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1b KEYNOTE-028 and Phase 2 KEYNOTE-158 studies in salivary gland carcinoma. ORR 5-12% across studies. FDA approved for MSI-H/TMB-H tumors. Systematic review and meta-analysis available. Low overall response rate limits enthusiasm

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR only 5-12% in unselected salivary gland cancer. Median OS 13-21 months. Durable responses seen in rare responders. Most CEPA tumors are immune-evasive. No specific data in CEPA subtype

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for multiple indications including tissue-agnostic MSI-H/TMB-H. Available at all oncology centers. Off-label use for salivary gland cancer feasible but insurance coverage may require prior authorization

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well tolerated. Immune-related adverse events (colitis, pneumonitis, hepatitis, thyroiditis, dermatitis) occur in 10-20% of patients. Manageable with corticosteroids in most cases. Better toxicity profile than cytotoxic chemotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient is HER2 negative; no PD-L1 status provided. CEPA generally has low PD-L1 expression and is immune-evasive. Response rates below 15% highlight immune-evasive nature of salivary gland malignancies. PLAG1 rearrangement has no known predictive value for immunotherapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody that binds PD-1 receptor on T cells, blocking interaction with PD-L1/PD-L2 ligands on tumor cells. This releases immune checkpoint inhibition, restoring T cell-mediated anti-tumor cytotoxicity. Responses depend on pre-existing anti-tumor immunity and PD-L1 expression.

key_evidence.study_name: KEYNOTE-028 Phase 1b — Pembrolizumab in Advanced Salivary Gland Carcinoma

biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS >= 1 (enrichment, not absolute requirement)
MSI-H or TMB-H for tissue-agnostic approval
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (tissue-agnostic for MSI-H/TMB-H; off-label for salivary gland cancer)

treatments.name: Cisplatin + Doxorubicin + Cyclophosphamide (CAP) Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trials with small sample sizes (13-22 patients). No Phase 3 randomized data. Multiple series confirm activity in advanced salivary gland carcinoma. Limited by rarity of tumor type

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 27-46% with median response duration of 7 months. Median OS approximately 12 months in advanced/metastatic setting. No evidence that adjuvant chemotherapy adds benefit to surgery plus RT for CEPA. Primarily used for recurrent/metastatic disease

accessibility.rationale: All three agents are generic, widely available, and inexpensive. Can be administered at any oncology center with infusion capability. Well-established dosing protocols

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: cisplatin nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity, doxorubicin cardiotoxicity (cumulative dose limit), cyclophosphamide hemorrhagic cystitis and myelosuppression. Grade 3-4 hematologic toxicity common. Requires close monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-driven — applicable to all salivary gland carcinomas. However, no specific enrichment expected from PLAG1 rearrangement. Ki-67 30% may indicate higher proliferative fraction susceptible to cytotoxic chemotherapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Triple cytotoxic combination: cisplatin forms DNA crosslinks inhibiting replication and transcription; doxorubicin intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II; cyclophosphamide is an alkylating agent that crosslinks DNA strands. Together they provide multi-mechanism cytotoxicity against rapidly dividing tumor cells.

key_evidence.study_name: CAP in Advanced Salivary Gland Cancer — Phase II
key_evidence.journal: Archives of Otolaryngology - Head and Neck Surgery

biomarker_requirements: None
treatments.availability: Widely available — all agents are generic and accessible globally

treatments.name: Lenvatinib (Multi-kinase Inhibitor / Anti-angiogenic Therapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial data in adenoid cystic carcinoma (32 patients). 16% ORR but 75% disease control rate and impressive 17.5-month median PFS. Primarily studied in ACC subtype but mechanism is relevant to other salivary gland carcinomas

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR only 16% but disease stabilization is common (75% DCR). Median PFS 17.5 months suggests meaningful disease control. Tumor shrinkage is uncommon but prolonged stable disease achieved. Limited data specifically in CEPA

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for thyroid cancer, hepatocellular carcinoma, renal cell carcinoma, and endometrial cancer. Off-label use for salivary gland cancer feasible. Oral administration is convenient

safety_profile.rationale: High toxicity rate: all-grade AEs in 96%, grade 3+ in 50%. Dose reduction required in 75% of patients. Hypertension, diarrhea, fatigue, proteinuria, palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia. Limits long-term tolerability

biomarker_match.rationale: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR, FGFR, PDGFR, RET, KIT. Not specific to PLAG1 rearrangement. Anti-angiogenic mechanism is relevant to highly vascularized tumors. No companion diagnostic required

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenvatinib is an oral multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFRa, RET, and KIT. It inhibits tumor angiogenesis and disrupts the tumor microenvironment by blocking multiple signaling pathways involved in tumor growth and vascular supply.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of Lenvatinib in Recurrent/Metastatic ACC

biomarker_requirements: None required
notable_side_effects: Dose reduction required in 75% of patients
treatments.availability: FDA approved for other indications; off-label for salivary gland cancer

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + Vorinostat (Immunotherapy + HDAC Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 clinical trial with 25 patients. Published data shows improvement over pembrolizumab monotherapy. Vorinostat as epigenetic modifier may enhance immunotherapy response. Small sample size limits conclusions

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 16% with median PFS 6.9 months and median OS 14 months. Improvement over pembrolizumab monotherapy ORR of 5-12%. Some durable responses observed. Still modest benefit in majority of patients

accessibility.rationale: Both agents individually FDA approved (pembrolizumab for other indications, vorinostat for CTCL). Combination use off-label for salivary gland cancer. May require clinical trial access or compassionate use

safety_profile.rationale: Combined toxicity of immune checkpoint inhibition and HDAC inhibition. Vorinostat adds thrombocytopenia, fatigue, GI toxicity. Manageable but additive adverse event profile compared to pembrolizumab alone

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker enrichment for CEPA with PLAG1 rearrangement. Epigenetic modifier may address immune-evasive phenotype. PD-L1 status unknown for this patient. Limited evidence in CEPA subtype

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Vorinostat is a histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitor that modifies chromatin structure, increasing expression of tumor antigens and immune-related genes. This epigenetic reprogramming may overcome the immune-evasive phenotype of salivary gland cancers, enhancing sensitivity to PD-1 blockade by pembrolizumab.
biomarker_requirements: None specified — potential enrichment with PD-L1 expression
treatments.availability: Off-label combination or clinical trial access

treatments.name: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab (Multi-kinase Inhibitor + Immunotherapy Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial completed in 2025 with 26 evaluable patients. Study positive for primary best overall response endpoint in adenoid cystic carcinoma subgroup. Grade 5 events observed raise safety concerns. Limited data in non-ACC salivary histologies

survival_benefit.rationale: Met primary endpoint for best overall response in ACC subgroup. Less promising for salivary duct carcinoma due to low response rate and grade 5 events. Combination aims to synergize anti-angiogenic and immune checkpoint mechanisms. Specific CEPA data not available

accessibility.rationale: Both agents FDA approved individually. Combination used primarily in clinical trial context for salivary gland cancer. Available at academic centers with trial access

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 5 (fatal) adverse events observed in the trial, raising significant safety concerns. Combined toxicity of lenvatinib (hypertension, proteinuria) and pembrolizumab (immune-related AEs) is substantial. Requires very close monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: No PLAG1-specific rationale. Anti-angiogenic plus immune checkpoint combination has broad anti-tumor mechanism. HER2 negative status does not impact this combination. Unknown PD-L1 status limits immune checkpoint predictive value

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combination of lenvatinib's anti-angiogenic and multi-kinase inhibition with pembrolizumab's PD-1 blockade. Lenvatinib may modulate the tumor immune microenvironment by reducing immunosuppressive regulatory T cells and tumor-associated macrophages, enhancing pembrolizumab efficacy. VEGF pathway inhibition normalizes tumor vasculature, improving immune cell infiltration.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab in Recurrent/Metastatic SGC

biomarker_requirements: None specified
treatments.availability: Clinical trial — phase II completed; not yet standard practice

treatments.name: Nivolumab + Ipilimumab (Dual Checkpoint Inhibitor Immunotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Ongoing clinical trial with results expected mid-2025. Dual checkpoint blockade rationale extrapolated from other tumor types. No published efficacy data specifically in salivary gland cancer for this combination. Nivolumab monotherapy showed only 4.2% ORR in SGC

survival_benefit.rationale: Nivolumab monotherapy ORR only 4.2% with 10.7-month median OS in SGC. Dual checkpoint inhibition may improve responses but salivary gland tumors are generally immune-evasive. Pending trial results

accessibility.rationale: Both agents FDA approved individually for multiple cancers. Combination approved for melanoma, RCC, NSCLC, and others. Off-label use for SGC or clinical trial enrollment required

safety_profile.rationale: Dual checkpoint inhibition has higher immune-related toxicity than monotherapy. Grade 3+ immune AEs occur in 30-40% of patients. Colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies. Requires experienced immune toxicity management

biomarker_match.rationale: No PLAG1-specific rationale. CEPA generally immune-evasive. Dual checkpoint strategy may overcome single-agent resistance. PD-L1 and TMB status unknown for this patient. Limited predictive biomarkers available

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab blocks PD-1 on T cells while ipilimumab blocks CTLA-4, releasing two distinct immune checkpoints. CTLA-4 blockade primarily enhances T cell priming in lymph nodes, while PD-1 blockade restores T cell effector function in the tumor microenvironment. The combination produces synergistic anti-tumor immune activation that may overcome the immune-evasive phenotype of salivary gland carcinomas.

key_evidence.study_name: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab in Recurrent/Metastatic SGC (Ongoing Trial)

biomarker_requirements: None specified — PD-L1 and TMB may be predictive
treatments.availability: Clinical trial enrollment or off-label use at academic centers

clinical_trials.title: Pembrolizumab + Vorinostat in Recurrent/Metastatic Salivary Gland Cancer
biomarker_requirements: Recurrent or metastatic salivary gland carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 16%, median PFS 6.9 months, median OS 14 months in 25 patients. Improved over pembrolizumab monotherapy

clinical_trials.title: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab in Recurrent/Metastatic Salivary Gland Cancer
biomarker_requirements: Recurrent or metastatic salivary gland carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Met primary best overall response endpoint. 26 evaluable patients. Active in ACC subgroup. Grade 5 events observed, limiting enthusiasm for non-ACC histologies

clinical_trials.title: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab in Recurrent or Metastatic Salivary Gland Carcinoma
biomarker_requirements: Recurrent or metastatic salivary gland carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Pending — evaluating dual checkpoint inhibition in immune-evasive salivary gland tumors

clinical_trials.title: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan in HER2-Expressing Recurrent/Metastatic Salivary Gland Cancer (MYTHOS)
biomarker_requirements: HER2-expressing (IHC 2+ or 3+)
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 68.4%, median PFS 15.9 months in 19 patients. Interstitial lung disease in 36.8%. Not applicable for this HER2-negative patient

clinical_trials.title: Goserelin Acetate + Pembrolizumab in AR-Positive Salivary Gland Carcinoma
biomarker_requirements: Androgen receptor positive salivary gland carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Pending — combining androgen deprivation with immune checkpoint inhibition

clinical_trials.title: Larotrectinib in NTRK Fusion-Positive Solid Tumors (NAVIGATE)
biomarker_requirements: NTRK1, NTRK2, or NTRK3 gene fusion
clinical_trials.early_results: 92% ORR in salivary gland cancer cohort. Median DOR 35.2 months. FDA approved based on combined trial data

clinical_trials.title: Amivantamab in Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma and Other Salivary Gland Cancers
biomarker_requirements: Salivary gland carcinoma — multiple histologies eligible
clinical_trials.early_results: Ongoing — bispecific EGFR/MET antibody in salivary gland cancers

combination_strategies.evidence_level: NCCN Guideline / Retrospective Series (highest available for rare tumor)
combination_strategies.rationale: Standard of care for stage III CEPA. Surgery achieves local control; adjuvant RT improves 5-year locoregional control from 49% to 75%. T3N1 disease with Ki-67 30% clearly warrants adjuvant RT. National Cancer Database analysis confirms survival benefit

combination_strategies.evidence_level: Retrospective / Extrapolated from H&N SCC guidelines
combination_strategies.rationale: National Cancer Database study showed no significant OS benefit from adding chemotherapy to adjuvant RT in major salivary gland cancer. However, for high-risk features (positive margins, extracapsular extension, T3N1 with Ki-67 30%), concurrent cisplatin may be considered on case-by-case basis. Evidence extrapolated from head and neck squamous cell carcinoma paradigm

Epigenetic reprogramming via HDAC inhibition upregulates tumor antigen presentation and immune-related gene expression, potentially overcoming the immune-evasive phenotype of salivary gland carcinomas. ORR improved from 5-12% (monotherapy) to 16% (combination). Median OS 14 months

VEGF pathway inhibition normalizes tumor vasculature and reduces immunosuppressive cells (Tregs, TAMs), creating a more permissive microenvironment for checkpoint inhibitor efficacy. Met primary endpoint in ACC subgroup but grade 5 events observed. Use with caution

NGS identifies actionable secondary molecular events in CEPA beyond PLAG1 rearrangement. Finding an NTRK fusion (ORR 92%), HER2 amplification (ORR 68%), or AR expression (ORR 42%) at diagnosis enables immediate targeted therapy if recurrence occurs. Proactive profiling prevents treatment delays

supportive_care.evidence: Systematic reviews and meta-analyses of RCTs demonstrate pre-habilitation and rehabilitation exercises reduce post-treatment dysphagia severity. Pilot data show regular jaw, tongue, lip, and laryngeal exercises improve speech and swallowing outcomes after oropharyngeal surgery. Prehabilitation during radiation maintains muscle function
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces aspiration pneumonia risk, improves swallowing function, prevents feeding tube dependence, enhances quality of life. Critical for parapharyngeal space surgery where CN IX, X, XII may be at risk. Early intervention produces significantly greater improvement in global measures of speech and swallowing

supportive_care.evidence: Evidence-based guidelines recommend 1.2-1.5 g protein/kg/day and 125 kJ/kg body weight/day during head and neck cancer treatment. Multidisciplinary nutritional screening, assessment, and intervention demonstrated improved nutritional status and quality of life. Weight loss during RT predicts worse outcomes
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains body weight and muscle mass during treatment, supports immune function, reduces treatment breaks, improves tolerance of radiation and chemotherapy. Prevents cancer cachexia and treatment-related sarcopenia

supportive_care.approach: Facial Nerve Rehabilitation and Reconstructive Surgery
supportive_care.evidence: If facial nerve sacrifice required during parapharyngeal space surgery, immediate nerve grafting (sural nerve or great auricular nerve) is standard. Gold weight insertion for eye protection. Dynamic (gracilis free flap with cross-facial nerve graft) or static (fascia lata sling) facial reanimation techniques for permanent paralysis. Physical therapy with neuromuscular retraining
supportive_care.benefit: Restores facial symmetry and function, protects cornea from exposure keratopathy, improves social interaction and quality of life. Immediate nerve grafting at time of resection provides best functional outcomes for facial nerve recovery

supportive_care.evidence: Head and neck cancer patients experience high rates of depression (20-40%), anxiety, body image disturbance, and social isolation. Visible surgical defects and functional impairments (speech, swallowing, facial movement) compound psychological distress. Cognitive behavioral therapy and support groups demonstrate benefit
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces depression and anxiety, improves treatment adherence and quality of life, addresses body image concerns related to facial nerve dysfunction and surgical scars. Supports return to social and occupational function

supportive_care.evidence: Pre-radiation dental evaluation and management prevents osteoradionecrosis and radiation caries. Fluoride trays, saliva substitutes, and regular dental monitoring reduce late dental complications. NCCN guidelines recommend comprehensive dental evaluation before head and neck radiation
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents osteoradionecrosis (risk with 60-66 Gy to mandible), reduces radiation caries, manages xerostomia, maintains oral health during and after radiation therapy. Extraction of compromised teeth before RT prevents catastrophic dental complications

supportive_care.evidence: Meta-analyses demonstrate structured exercise programs during and after cancer treatment improve fatigue, physical function, and quality of life. Head and neck cancer-specific programs include neck and shoulder rehabilitation (post-neck dissection), progressive resistance training, and aerobic exercise
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces cancer-related fatigue, maintains muscle strength and range of motion post-neck dissection, improves cardiovascular fitness, supports mental health. Progressive resistance training preserves lean body mass during treatment
